data.title: Risk aversion outweighs inflation concerns, causing US Treasury yields to fall.
data.description: Despite rising oil prices, U.S. Treasury yields fell in Asian trading as bond investors gradually shifted their focus from inflation fears stemming from the Middle East wars to growth risks. Elmar Voelker, senior fixed-income analyst at Baden-Württemberg Bank, stated in a report that the disruptions and damage to the energy sector so far are likely to have lasting effects and could ripple through other sectors of the economy. The bank projects that economies on both sides of the Atlantic will suffer approximately 0.25 percentage points of growth loss this year compared to its previous lead scenario. Tradeweb data shows that the two-year U.S. Treasury yield fell 3.9 basis points to 3.875%, and the ten-year U.S. Treasury yield fell 5.2 basis points to 4.387%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw net outflows of $296 million last week, with BlackRock's IBIT experiencing the largest net outflow at $158 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $296 million last week (March 23 to March 27, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (IBIT) experienced the largest net outflow last week, with a weekly net outflow of $158 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow is currently $63.1 billion. The Bitwise ETF (BITB) followed, with a weekly net outflow of $68.2939 million. BITB's historical total net inflow is currently $2.07 billion. The Fidelity ETF (FBTC) saw the largest net inflow last week, with a weekly net inflow of $46.8825 million. FBTC's historical total net inflow is currently $10.99 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $84.77 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) is 6.42%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $55.93 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw net outflows of $207 million last week, with BlackRock's ETHA experiencing the largest net outflow at $285 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $207 million last week (March 23 to March 27, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net outflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a weekly net outflow of $285 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow is currently $11.63 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETH, with a weekly net outflow of $24.9 million. ETH's historical total net inflow is currently $1.82 billion. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net inflow last week was BlackRock ETF ETHB, with a weekly net inflow of $141 million. ETHB's historical total net inflow is currently $301 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $11.32 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) is 4.72%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.52 billion.

data.title: Trade.xyz Sees Record High in Traditional Asset Open Interest
data.description:
On March 30, Trade.xyz reported a significant increase in open interest for traditional assets, according to BlockBeats On-chain Detection. The contracts, deployed through HIP-3 on the Hyperliquid platform, reached $1.65 billion in daily open interest. Despite a slight decline in trading volume to $2.07 billion, the open interest accounted for over 90% of the HIP-3 market share.

Additionally, geopolitical tensions have influenced the oil market, maintaining its position as the leading traditional asset on the platform. The combined open interest for WTI and Brent crude oil surpassed $774 million, with a trading volume of $497 million.

data.title: Bittensor's TAO Rebounds as Market Sentiment Improves
data.description: Bittensor's TAO has experienced a significant rebound, climbing to approximately $330 from a February low of $154. According to NS3.AI, this upward movement coincides with CryptoQuant's 90-day spot taker CVD turning positive. Over the past week, TAO has increased by more than 20%, boosting its market capitalization to around $3.17 billion.

data.title: The Ethereum Foundation recently staked $46.2 million worth of ETH.
data.description: According to Arkham, the Ethereum Foundation staked $46.2 million worth of ETH today, marking the highest amount of ETH ever staked by the Ethereum Foundation.

data.title: Bank of Japan Considers Potential Rate Hike Amid Economic Stability
data.description: The Bank of Japan has indicated the possibility of further monetary tightening, with traders now estimating a 69% likelihood of a rate hike at the upcoming policy meeting on April 28. According to NS3.AI, a summary from the Monetary Policy Meeting released on March 30 revealed that one of the nine board members expressed support for raising rates if the economy remains stable and avoids a significant downturn.

data.title: Binance: The R2 Protocol (R2) airdrop threshold is 252 Alpha Credits.
data.description: Binance Alpha will list R2 Protocol (R2), with trading commencing on March 30, 2026 at 10:00 AM UTC. Users with at least 252 Binance Alpha Points can claim an airdrop of 750 R2 tokens on a first-come, first-served basis. If the reward pool is not fully distributed, the points threshold will automatically decrease by 5 points every 5 minutes. Please note that claiming the airdrop will cost 15 Binance Alpha Points. Users must confirm their claim on the Alpha event page within 24 hours; otherwise, they will be considered to have forfeited their claim.

data.title: Polymarket Trader Profits from UFC Result Error
data.description: A Polymarket trader experienced a significant windfall after a UFC announcer mistakenly announced the wrong winner in a heavyweight fight. According to NS3.AI, the trader initially invested $676 in Tyrell Fortune shares at a price of one cent each. The error was quickly corrected, but not before the trader's investment surged to $67,608.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Strategists Recommend Buying Five-Year U.S. Treasuries
data.description:
Morgan Stanley strategists have advised investors to purchase five-year U.S. Treasuries at a yield of 4.06%, according to Jin10. Tradeweb data shows the latest transaction price for the five-year Treasury yield at 4.022%. The strategists also suggest betting on a steepening curve from the seven-year to the 30-year U.S. Treasuries, entering at a level of 71 basis points.

The strategists noted that since February 27, U.S. Treasury yields have risen alongside energy prices. However, they believe that the recent pricing trends in the one-year/one-year U.S. CPI inflation swap indicate increasing downside risks to growth, which stock indices are beginning to reflect. They argue that if energy prices continue to rise, the risks to growth will further escalate.

data.title: S&P: Japan's Long-Term Rating Outlook Shows Resilience Amid Geopolitical Risks
data.description: According to Jin10, S&P has indicated that Japan's long-term rating outlook reflects resilience in its nominal GDP and tax revenue growth despite the risks posed by geopolitical tensions. The agency highlights that Japan's economic fundamentals remain strong, allowing it to withstand external pressures. This assessment underscores the country's ability to maintain stability in its financial metrics, even as geopolitical challenges persist.

data.title: Westpac Increases Rate Hike Expectations Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: Westpac has revised its interest rate expectations for the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) due to worsening inflation prospects. According to Jin10, the bank now anticipates additional rate hikes in June and August, alongside the previously forecasted increase in May. Westpac's projections suggest that the official cash rate will reach 4.85% by the end of the year. Luci Ellis, Westpac's Chief Economist, noted that this adjustment reflects a longer and slower recovery from previously assumed fuel supply disruptions.

data.title: Central Banks in Europe and Japan Adopt Hawkish Stance Amid Energy Concerns
data.description: According to Jin10, Lei Wang from Thornburg Investment Management highlighted in a report that the Bank of England, European Central Bank, and Bank of Japan have adopted a more aggressive hawkish stance due to their higher dependency on energy imports compared to the United States. The portfolio manager noted that the sharp rise in oil and natural gas prices has significantly contributed to overall inflation, causing concern among policymakers about the initial energy shock. Wang also pointed out the secondary effects, including wage levels, pricing behavior, and inflation expectations.

data.title: Study Reveals Tokyo-Based Validators Offer Faster Transactions for Hyperliquid Traders
data.description:
Glassnode research indicates that Hyperliquid's validator cluster is located in Amazon Web Services' Tokyo region, providing Tokyo traders with approximately 200 milliseconds faster order round-trip times compared to participants from Europe and the United States, according to PANews. This advantage results in better queue positions and transaction quality for those near the infrastructure.

The study shows that the median round-trip time for orders placed from AWS Tokyo nodes to confirmation is approximately 884 milliseconds, with only 5 milliseconds attributed to network transmission and the remainder to server processing time. In contrast, the round-trip time from Virginia's Ashburn node increases to about 1079 milliseconds. Unlike traditional financial markets, which use measures like cable length equalization and speed bumps to eliminate geographical advantages, decentralized markets lack similar safeguards. As institutional capital enters DeFi, a competitive landscape where speed determines transaction priority is emerging.

data.title: Tron Inc. Increases TRX Holdings to Over 689 Million
data.description: Tron Inc. has announced an increase in its TRX holdings, acquiring 156,819 TRX at an average price of $0.3188. According to PANews, this acquisition brings the company's total TRX treasury holdings to over 689 million. Tron Inc. stated its commitment to continue increasing its TRX holdings to enhance long-term shareholder value.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Based FDV to Surpass $50 Million Within a Day of Launch
data.description: Polymarket has projected a 91% probability that Based FDV will exceed $50 million within one day of its launch. According to Foresight News, the likelihood of it surpassing $75 million within the same timeframe is currently reported at 80%, while the chance of reaching $100 million stands at 50%. The trading volume for this prediction event has already surpassed $4 million.

BIT: ETH breaks below key support level, bearish flag pattern may continue the downward trend; 3. data.title: Market Sentiment: Growth Concerns May Limit U.S. Treasury Yield Rise
data.description: TD Securities strategists have indicated that U.S. Treasury yields may remain elevated in the short term as the Federal Reserve maintains a cautious stance. According to Jin10, they noted that while inflation expectations are rising, the market has begun to factor in rate hike expectations, overlooking potential downside risks to economic growth. The strategists emphasized that concerns over slowing growth could restrict further increases in yields. Tradeweb data shows that during the Asian trading session, U.S. Treasury yields declined, with the 10-year yield dropping by 4.8 basis points to 4.391%.

data.title: Israel's Central Bank Expected to Maintain Interest Rates Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: Israel's central bank is anticipated to keep interest rates unchanged for the second time in a row, as ongoing US-Israeli attacks on Iran continue to drive inflation worries. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the geopolitical tensions that are influencing economic decisions. The central bank's decision reflects the complex interplay between regional security issues and economic stability, as policymakers navigate the challenges posed by external pressures on inflation. The situation underscores the delicate balance central banks must maintain in responding to both domestic and international factors affecting their economies.

data.title: Thailand's Prime Minister to Announce New Cabinet Amid Middle East Crisis
data.description: Thailand's Prime Minister Anutin is set to unveil a new cabinet list, a move that will grant him greater authority to tackle challenges arising from the Middle East crisis, which threatens the growth prospects of the Southeast Asian nation. According to Jin10, Anutin informed reporters that a list of 35 ministers has been prepared and will be submitted for royal approval on Monday, following background checks by various agencies. Local media reports suggest that the announcement may be made on April 7, detailing economic stimulus and debt management plans, as well as steps to enhance Thailand's competitiveness and promote inclusive growth. The statement is also expected to include measures to increase revenue and address natural disasters and national security issues. The Prime Minister has faced criticism due to fuel shortages, soaring prices, and supply chain challenges resulting from the Gulf conflict. The ripple effects of the war have led some economists to lower Thailand's growth forecasts, as it impacts the country's key export and tourism sectors.
